operating_state: Net revenues increased 12% to $16.8 billion driven by strong performance across all segments, with equity trading benefiting from market volatility and wealth management generating elevated asset management revenues. Total assets expanded to $1.35 trillion from $1.22 trillion at year-end, primarily reflecting increased trading positions and securities financing activities.
cost_structure: Total non-interest expenses rose to $12.0 billion from $10.9 billion, with compensation increasing 11% due to higher formulaic payouts and deferred compensation expenses while non-compensation costs rose 9% from execution-related and technology spend, maintaining 71% efficiency ratio. Credit loss provisions increased to $331 million for six months from $70 million prior year, reflecting portfolio growth and slower GDP growth assumptions.
material_events: The Board elected Lynn J. Good as independent director and shareholders approved amended equity compensation plan adding 50 million shares. The company increased quarterly dividend to $1.00 from $0.925 and repurchased $2.0 billion in stock for six months. The firm reached settlement with Dutch tax authorities and announced new $20 billion share repurchase authorization beginning Q3 2025.
macro_exposures: Credit exposure increased to $452 billion driven by corporate loans and secured lending growth, with allowance models incorporating slower GDP growth assumptions. The firm maintains substantial interest rate exposure through borrowings at fair value and expanded foreign exchange derivative notionals. Commercial real estate exposure continues experiencing office sector stress driving charge-offs.
forward_outlook: Management expects Stress Capital Buffer to decrease to 5.1% from 6.0% effective October 2025, reducing required CET1 ratio to 12.6%. The firm maintains ROTCE goal of 20% under normal market conditions while acknowledging potential impacts from geopolitical uncertainty and trade policy changes. Base case economic scenario assumes growth slowdown in 2025 followed by gradual improvement in 2026.
uncertainty_profile: Dutch criminal authorities announced intention to bring charges against firm subsidiaries for allegedly filing false tax returns for 2007-2012, which the firm disputes. The firm faces continued volatility in fair value measurements and potential collateral requirements for credit rating downgrades. Regulatory capital requirements face potential changes from proposed Federal Reserve modifications to capital framework requirements.
